objective: Japanese companies with a recent, concrete AI initiative that involves or would clearly benefit from a web search API (agents, RAG, research assistants, market/news intelligence)

hard_criteria.text: Has a specific, dated (within the last 18 months, i.e. since January 2025), publicly documented AI initiative — a named product, deployment, business unit, or funded program; not a vague 'exploring AI' statement.

soft_criteria.text: The initiative plausibly needs live web data or search: agents, RAG over external content, research/intel tools, news monitoring. Grade STRICTLY — 'strong' only if the initiative itself retrieves or reasons over web-scale external content; do not stretch low-relevance initiatives.
Signals they build in-house and buy APIs: engineering hiring, tech blog, existing API vendor usage.
Executive sponsorship, budget, or company-level strategy attached to the initiative.

columns.desc: one sentence: the concrete way a web search API fits — no stretching
2-3 sentences on what the initiative is

segments.label: jp_tech_press
segments.focus: Japanese tech/business press on enterprise AI initiatives — Nikkei, ITmedia, ASCII, PR Times releases; search in Japanese (AI エージェント, 生成AI 導入, RAG).

segments.label: ai_products
segments.focus: Japanese companies that shipped AI-powered products with search/agent/RAG features — product pages, launch posts, API docs.

segments.label: partnerships
segments.focus: Japanese companies in announced partnerships with LLM vendors (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google) or cloud AI programs.
